_comment: Machine-readable regulatory rules for WarRoom. Phase 3 domain data. Compliance's check_regulatory_triggers matches an incident's affected data_classes against each rule's trigger.data_classes; a match returns the obligation, the clock duration, and whether evidence must be preserved (which is what blocks a wipe).

rules.name: GDPR Article 33 — Personal data breach notification

trigger.conditions: Confirmed or suspected breach of personal data (confidentiality, integrity, or availability).

rules.obligation: Notify the lead supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware. If notification exceeds 72h, document the reasons for delay.

rules.name: SEC Item 1.05 — Material cybersecurity incident (Form 8-K)

trigger.conditions: Cybersecurity incident determined to be material to a reasonable investor.

rules.obligation: File a Form 8-K describing the material aspects of the incident within 4 business days of the materiality determination.

rules.name: HIPAA Breach Notification Rule

trigger.conditions: Unsecured protected health information (PHI) acquired, accessed, used, or disclosed.

rules.obligation: Notify affected individuals and HHS without unreasonable delay and no later than 60 days from discovery.
rules.authority: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (OCR)

rules.name: Internal forensic evidence retention policy

trigger.conditions: Any incident that may lead to regulatory notification or litigation.

rules.obligation: Preserve a forensically sound disk image and volatile-memory capture of affected hosts BEFORE any remediation that destroys state (wipe, reimage). Destroying evidence may constitute spoliation.
